Tests the immutability of `Number.NaN` by attempting to assign it a value and checking with `isNaN`.

# Purpose
This code modifies the `Number` object by setting `Number.NaN` to `0`, which is not standard behavior in JavaScript. It then uses an `assert` statement to check if `isNaN(Number.NaN)` returns `true`. This code demonstrates a test case to verify the behavior of the `isNaN` function when `Number.NaN` is set to a non-standard value. The code is a short script that focuses on testing the behavior of JavaScript's `isNaN` function in a specific scenario.
